challenges.title: Style Nested List Items
challenges.description: Create a nested list and use the child combinator to style only top-level list items differently from nested ones.
requirements: A nested ul > li > ul structure
Top-level li items styled with bold text
Nested li items styled with normal weight and different color
Use child combinator (>) for top-level items
Use descendant combinator (space) for nested items

solution.html:
• Fruits
    • Apple
    • Banana
    • Orange
  • Vegetables
    • Carrot
    • Broccoli
solution.explanation: ul > li selects direct children (top-level items), styling them bold. ul ul li selects all nested items, overriding with normal weight and a different color.

challenges.title: Card Component with Combinators
challenges.description: Style a card component using only combinators (no extra classes). The card has a header, content, and footer.
requirements: Card div with inner header, content, footer divs
Header: larger font, border-bottom
Content: padding, different background
Footer: smaller font, border-top, muted color
Use only combinators (not classes on inner elements)

solution.html:
Card Header
This is the main content area of the card.
Card Footer
solution.explanation: Uses child combinator (>) combined with pseudo-classes (:first-child, :last-child) to target specific child divs without adding individual classes.

challenges.title: Article Styling with Sibling Combinators
challenges.description: Style an article using sibling combinators. Each h2 should be followed by a styled intro paragraph, and all subsequent siblings should have different styling.
requirements: Article with h2 headings and paragraphs
Paragraph immediately after h2: larger font, different color
All remaining paragraphs after h2: indented with left border
Use + and ~ combinators appropriately

solution.html:
Introduction

This intro paragraph is immediately after h2 - special styling!

This is a regular paragraph after the intro.

Another regular paragraph with left border.

Main Content

This intro paragraph for the second section gets special styling too!

Following paragraphs get the border treatment.
solution.explanation: h2 + p selects the immediately following paragraph (special intro). h2 ~ p selects all following paragraphs (border). The + rule overrides the ~ rule for the first paragraph.

challenges.title: Navigation with Active State Using Combinators
challenges.description: Create a navigation bar where hovering over an item reveals its submenu. Use combinators to style active/current page indicators.
requirements: Horizontal nav with dropdown submenus
Submenu appears on hover using child combinator
Active page indicator using adjacent sibling
No JavaScript allowed

solution.html:
• Home
  • Services
    • Web Design
    • Development
  • About
  • Contact
solution.explanation: Uses child combinator (>) throughout: nav > ul selects direct nav children, li:hover > ul selects submenu on hover, li:hover > a styles the hovered link.

challenges.title: Combinator-Based Card Grid with Hover Effects
challenges.description: Build a card grid where hover effects and spacing are controlled entirely through CSS combinators. Include cards with images, titles, and descriptions.
requirements: Grid of cards (3x2) using combinators
Card structure: card > img + .content > h3 + p
Hover: card:hover > img scales up
Spacing: card + card adds left margin (first card in row un-affected)
Content title: first-child styled differently
Last card in each row: no right margin
No extra classes on inner elements

solution.html:
Image 1

Card Title 1

Description

Image 2

Card Title 2

Description

Image 3

Card Title 3

Description

Image 4

Card Title 4

Description

Image 5

Card Title 5

Description

Image 6

Card Title 6

Description
solution.explanation: Uses: child combinator (>) for card structure, :first-child and :last-child pseudo-classes to target inner elements, :hover > for hover effects, :nth-child with combinators for row targeting, adjacent sibling for card spacing.
